poems.title: ಬಾರೋ ನನ್ನ ತೋಟಕೆ
poems.kannada_text:
ಬಾರೋ ನನ್ನ ತೋಟಕೆ ಬಾರೋ
ಹೂವಿನ ನಾಡಿಗೆ ಬಾರೋ
ಮಲ್ಲಿಗೆ ಮಲ್ಲಿಗೆ ಅರಳಿದೆ
ತಂಗಾಳಿ ತಂಗಾಳಿ ಬೀಸಿದೆ
poems.english_meaning:
Come to my garden, come
Come to the land of flowers
The jasmine has bloomed
The cool breeze is blowing
poems.bhavartha: ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಕವಿ ಕುವೆಂಪು ಪ್ರಕೃತಿಯ ಸೌಂದರ್ಯವನ್ನು ವರ್ಣಿಸುತ್ತಾರೆ.

poems.title: ನಿತ್ಯೋತ್ಸವ
poems.kannada_text:
ಹುಟ್ಟುವುದೇ ಹಬ್ಬ ಬದುಕುವುದೇ ಹಬ್ಬ
ಸಾಯುವುದೂ ಹಬ್ಬವೇ ಆಗಬೇಕು
ಪ್ರತಿ ದಿನವೂ ಹೊಸ ಬೆಳಕು
ಪ್ರತಿ ಕ್ಷಣವೂ ಹೊಸ ಸುಖ
poems.english_meaning:
Birth itself is a festival, living itself is a festival
Even death must become a festival
Every day is a new light
Every moment is a new joy
poems.bhavartha: ಕುವೆಂಪು ಅವರು ಜೀವನದ ಪ್ರತಿಯೊಂದು ಕ್ಷಣವನ್ನು ಉತ್ಸವವಾಗಿ ಆಚರಿಸಬೇಕು ಎಂದು ಹೇಳುತ್ತಾರೆ.

poems.title: ಓ ನನ್ನ ಚೇತನ
poems.kannada_text:
ಓ ನನ್ನ ಚೇತನ ಆಗು ನೀ ಅನಿಕೇತನ
ಬೆಳಕಿನ ಹಕ್ಕಿಯಾಗು ಗಗನದಲಿ
ಸಾಗರದ ಆಳಕ್ಕಿಳಿ ಮಿಂಚಿನ ಸಂಚಾರಿ
ವಿಶ್ವದ ನಾಗರಿಕ ನೀನಾಗು
poems.english_meaning:
O my spirit, become homeless and free
Become a bird of light in the sky
Dive into the depths of the ocean
Become a citizen of the universe
poems.bhavartha: ಕುವೆಂಪು ಅವರ ವಿಶ್ವಮಾನವ ತತ್ವದ ಸಾರ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿದೆ.

poems.title: ಮನುಜ ಮತ
poems.kannada_text:
ಜಾತಿ ಮತ ಬೇಡ ನಮಗೆ
ಮನುಜ ಮತವೊಂದೇ ಸಾಕು
ಎಲ್ಲರೂ ಒಂದೇ ದೇವರ ಮಕ್ಕಳು
ಪ್ರೇಮವೇ ಧರ್ಮ ನಮ್ಮದು
poems.english_meaning:
We do not need caste or religion
Only the religion of humanity is enough
All are children of one God
Love is our only dharma
poems.bhavartha: ಕುವೆಂಪು ಅವರ ವಿಶ್ವಮಾನವ ತತ್ವದ ಅಭಿವ್ಯಕ್ತಿ ಈ ಕವಿತೆ.

poems.title: ಹಕ್ಕಿ ಹಾರುತಿದೆ
poems.kannada_text:
ಹಕ್ಕಿ ಹಾರುತಿದೆ ನೋಡಿದಿರಾ
ದೂರ ದೂರ ಹಾರುತಿದೆ
ಮೇಘದ ಮೇಲೆ ಕುಳಿತಂತೆ
ಆಕಾಶದಲ್ಲಿ ಮಿಂಚಿದಂತೆ
poems.english_meaning:
Did you see the bird flying
Flying far and farther away
As if sitting on a cloud
Flashing like lightning in the sky
poems.bhavartha: ಬೇಂದ್ರೆ ಅವರು ಹಕ್ಕಿಯ ಹಾರಾಟವನ್ನು ಮಾನವ ಆಕಾಂಕ್ಷೆಯ ಸಂಕೇತವಾಗಿ ಚಿತ್ರಿಸಿದ್ದಾರೆ.

poems.title: ನಾದಲೀಲೆ
poems.kannada_text:
ನಾದವೇ ನನ್ನ ದೇವರು
ಲಯವೇ ನನ್ನ ಜೀವನ
ಸ್ವರದಲ್ಲಿ ಸ್ವರ್ಗ ಕಾಣುವೆ
ರಾಗದಲ್ಲಿ ರಾಮನ ಕಾಣುವೆ
poems.english_meaning:
Sound is my God
Rhythm is my life
In melody I see heaven
In raga I see Lord Rama
poems.bhavartha: ಬೇಂದ್ರೆ ಅವರ ಸಂಗೀತ ಪ್ರೇಮ ಮತ್ತು ಆಧ್ಯಾತ್ಮಿಕ ಚಿಂತನೆ ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಒಂದಾಗಿವೆ.

poems.title: ಅಮ್ಮನ ಮಡಿಲು
poems.kannada_text:
ಅಮ್ಮನ ಮಡಿಲೇ ಸ್ವರ್ಗ
ಅಮ್ಮನ ಮಾತೇ ವೇದ
ಅಮ್ಮನ ಕಣ್ಣೀರು ಗಂಗೆ
ಅಮ್ಮನ ನಗು ಸೂರ್ಯ
poems.english_meaning:
Mother's lap is heaven
Mother's words are the Vedas
Mother's tears are the Ganga
Mother's smile is the sun
poems.bhavartha: ತಾಯಿಯ ಮಹಿಮೆಯನ್ನು ಅನನ್ಯವಾಗಿ ಚಿತ್ರಿಸಿರುವ ಕವಿತೆ.

poems.title: ಭಾರತ ಮಾತೆ
poems.kannada_text:
ಭಾರತ ಮಾತೆಯ ಜಯವಾಗಲಿ
ನಮ್ಮ ನಾಡು ಶ್ರೇಷ್ಠವಾಗಲಿ
ಜ್ಞಾನದ ಬೆಳಕು ಹರಡಲಿ
ಐಕ್ಯತೆ ಮನೆ ಮಾಡಲಿ
poems.english_meaning:
Let Mother India be victorious
Let our nation be great
Let the light of knowledge spread
Let unity dwell in every home
poems.bhavartha: ಗೋಕಾಕ್ ಅವರ ರಾಷ್ಟ್ರಪ್ರೇಮದ ಅಭಿವ್ಯಕ್ತಿ ಈ ಕವಿತೆ.

poems.title: ಕನ್ನಡ ತಾಯಿ
poems.kannada_text:
ಕನ್ನಡ ನಾಡಿನ ತಾಯಿ ನೀನು
ನಿನ್ನ ಭಾಷೆ ಮಧುರ ಕನ್ನಡ
ಶ್ರೀಗಂಧದ ನಾಡಿನ ತಾಯಿ
ನಿನಗೆ ನನ್ನ ನಮಸ್ಕಾರ
poems.english_meaning:
You are the mother of the Kannada land
Your language is sweet Kannada
Mother of the sandalwood land
My salutations to you
poems.bhavartha: ಕನ್ನಡ ನಾಡು ಮತ್ತು ಭಾಷೆಯ ಮೇಲಿನ ಗೋಕಾಕ್ ಅವರ ಅಭಿಮಾನ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಪ್ರಕಟವಾಗಿದೆ.

poems.title: ಮನಸಿನ ತೊಳಲಾಟ
poems.kannada_text:
ಮನಸು ಎಲ್ಲಿ ಹೋಗಬೇಕು
ಯಾವ ದಾರಿ ಹಿಡಿಯಬೇಕು
ಸತ್ಯ ಸುಳ್ಳಿನ ನಡುವೆ
ಮನುಷ್ಯ ತಡಕಾಡುತ್ತಾನೆ
poems.english_meaning:
Where should the mind go
Which path should it take
Between truth and falsehood
A human being gropes in the dark
poems.bhavartha: ಅನಂತಮೂರ್ತಿ ಅವರ ಆಧುನಿಕ ಮನೋವಿಶ್ಲೇಷಣೆ ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಕಾಣುತ್ತದೆ.

poems.title: ರಂಗಭೂಮಿ
poems.kannada_text:
ಜೀವನ ಒಂದು ರಂಗಭೂಮಿ
ನಾವೆಲ್ಲ ಪಾತ್ರಧಾರಿಗಳು
ನಮ್ಮ ಪಾತ್ರ ನಾವು ಆಡಬೇಕು
ಪರದೆ ಬೀಳುವ ಮೊದಲು
poems.english_meaning:
Life is a stage
We are all actors
We must play our roles
Before the curtain falls
poems.bhavartha: ಕಾರ್ನಾಡ ಅವರ ನಾಟ್ಯ ಸಂಬಂಧಿ ಕವಿತೆ.

poems.title: ಜಾನಪದದ ಜೀವ
poems.kannada_text:
ಜಾನಪದ ಹಾಡು ಮನಸ್ಸಿಗೆ ಮುದ
ಹಳ್ಳಿಯ ದನಿ ಆತ್ಮಕ್ಕೆ ನೆಲ
ತಾಯ್ನಾಡಿನ ಸೊಗಡು ಉಸಿರಲಿ ಉಳಿಯಲಿ
ಕನ್ನಡ ಸಂಸ್ಕೃತಿ ಚಿರ ಜೀವಿಯಾಗಲಿ
poems.english_meaning:
Folk songs bring joy to the mind
The village voice grounds the soul
Let the fragrance of the motherland live in every breath
Let Kannada culture live forever
poems.bhavartha: ಕಂಬಾರ ಅವರ ಜಾನಪದ ಪ್ರೇಮ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಮೂರ್ತಗೊಂಡಿದೆ.

poems.title: ಆದಿಕವಿಯ ನುಡಿ
poems.kannada_text:
ಗಜಗಾಮಿನಿ ಮೆಲ್ಲನೆ ನಡೆದಳು
ಚಂದ್ರಮುಖಿ ಬೆಳಕ ಚೆಲ್ಲಿದಳು
ಕವಿ ಪಂಪನ ಕಲ್ಪನೆ ಮೂಡಿತು
ಕನ್ನಡ ಕಾವ್ಯ ಚಿಗುರಿ ಬೆಳೆಯಿತು
poems.english_meaning:
The elephant-gaited one walked gently
The moon-faced one spread her light
Poet Pampa's imagination bloomed
Kannada poetry sprouted and grew
poems.bhavartha: ಆದಿಕವಿ ಪಂಪನ ಕಾವ್ಯ ಪ್ರತಿಭೆಯನ್ನು ಸ್ಮರಿಸುವ ಕವಿತೆ.

words.ಗಜಗಾಮಿನಿ: One who walks like an elephant

poems.title: ವೀರ ರಣ್ಣನ ಕರೆ
poems.kannada_text:
ಶಸ್ತ್ರವ ಹಿಡಿ ವೀರ ಮರ್ದನ
ಧರ್ಮದ ಕಾಯ್ದು ನಿಲ್ಲು ಸದನ
ರಣದಲಿ ಗೆಲ್ಲು ನಿನ್ನ ಗೌರವ
ಕನ್ನಡ ನಾಡಿಗೆ ತರು ವಿಜಯ
poems.english_meaning:
Hold the weapon, O brave warrior
Stand guard protecting righteousness
Win your honor on the battlefield
Bring victory to the Kannada land
poems.bhavartha: ರನ್ನನ ಗದಾಯುದ್ಧದ ವೀರರಸದ ಪ್ರತಿಧ್ವನಿ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿದೆ.

poems.title: ಶಿವ ಶರಣರ ವಚನ
poems.kannada_text:
ಕಾಯಕವೇ ಕೈಲಾಸ
ಭಕ್ತಿಯೇ ಬದುಕಿನ ಸಾರ
ಶಿವನ ಸ್ಮರಣೆಯೇ ಮೋಕ್ಷ
ಪ್ರೀತಿಯೇ ಪರಮ ಧರ್ಮ
poems.english_meaning:
Work itself is Kailasa
Devotion is the essence of life
Remembering Shiva is liberation
Love is the supreme dharma
poems.bhavartha: ಬಸವಣ್ಣನ ಕಾಯಕ ತತ್ವ ಮತ್ತು ಭಕ್ತಿ ಮಾರ್ಗ ಈ ವಚನದಲ್ಲಿ ಸ್ಪಷ್ಟವಾಗಿ ಕಾಣುತ್ತದೆ.

poems.title: ಅಕ್ಕನ ಅರಿವು
poems.kannada_text:
ಚೆನ್ನಮಲ್ಲಿಕಾರ್ಜುನನ ಪ್ರೀತಿಯಲಿ
ಜಗವ ಮರೆತೆ ನಾನು
ಆತ್ಮವೇ ಶಿವನ ದೇಗುಲ
ಒಳಗಿನ ಬೆಳಕೇ ದೇವರು
poems.english_meaning:
In the love of Chennamallikarjuna
I forgot the world
The soul itself is Shiva's temple
The inner light itself is God
poems.bhavartha: ಅಕ್ಕಮಹಾದೇವಿಯ ಆಧ್ಯಾತ್ಮಿಕ ಪ್ರೇಮ ಮತ್ತು ಒಳಗಿನ ದೇವರ ಅರಿವು ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಮೂಡಿದೆ.

poems.title: ಮಳೆಯ ಹಾಡು
poems.kannada_text:
ಮಳೆ ಬಂತು ಮಳೆ ಬಂತು
ಭೂಮಿ ತಣಿದಳು
ರೈತನ ಮನ ಅರಳಿತು
ಹಸಿರು ಹೊದ್ದಿತು ನೆಲ
poems.english_meaning:
The rain came, the rain came
The earth was quenched
The farmer's heart blossomed
The land was covered in green
poems.bhavartha: ಬೇಂದ್ರೆ ಅವರ ಈ ಕವಿತೆ ಮಳೆಯ ಮಹತ್ವವನ್ನು ಸಾದರಪಡಿಸುತ್ತದೆ.

poems.title: ಕಾಡಿನ ಕರೆ
poems.kannada_text:
ಕಾಡು ಕರೆಯುತಿದೆ ಬಾ ಎಂದು
ಮರಗಳು ಕೈಬೀಸಿ ಕರೆಯuತಿವೆ
ಹಕ್ಕಿಗಳ ಹಾಡು ಕೇಳು
ನದಿಯ ನಾದ ಆಲಿಸು
poems.english_meaning:
The forest is calling, come it says
The trees are waving and calling
Listen to the song of birds
Hear the melody of the river
poems.bhavartha: ಪ್ರಕೃತಿಯ ಮಡಿಲಿಗೆ ಮರಳಬೇಕೆಂಬ ಕವಿಯ ಆಶಯ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ವ್ಯಕ್ತವಾಗಿದೆ.

poems.title: ನದಿಯ ದಾರಿ
poems.kannada_text:
ನದಿ ತನ್ನ ದಾರಿ ತಾನೇ ಮಾಡಿಕೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತದೆ
ಅಡ್ಡಿಗಳನ್ನು ದಾಟಿ ಮುಂದೆ ಹೋಗುತ್ತದೆ
ಮಾನವನೂ ಹಾಗೇ ಆಗಬೇಕು
ಸವಾಲು ಗೆದ್ದು ಗುರಿ ಮುಟ್ಟಬೇಕು
poems.english_meaning:
The river makes its own way
It crosses obstacles and moves forward
So too should humans
Overcoming challenges to reach the goal
poems.bhavartha: ಕಾರ್ನಾಡ ಅವರು ನದಿಯ ನಿರಂತರ ಪ್ರವಾಹವನ್ನು ಮಾನವ ಜೀವನದ ಪ್ರತೀಕವಾಗಿ ಬಳಸಿದ್ದಾರೆ.

poems.title: ಕರಿಮಣಿ
poems.kannada_text:
ಕರಿಮಣಿ ಕೊರಳಲ್ಲಿ ಹೊಳೆಯುತ್ತದೆ
ಪ್ರೇಮದ ಸಂಕೇತ ಅದು
ಎರಡು ಜೀವ ಒಂದಾದಾಗ
ಜೀವನ ಹೂವಾಗಿ ಅರಳುತ್ತದೆ
poems.english_meaning:
The black beads shine around the neck
They are the symbol of love
When two souls become one
Life blossoms like a flower
poems.bhavartha: ಕಂಬಾರ ಅವರ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕ ಗ್ರಾಮೀಣ ಪರಂಪರೆಯ ಚಿತ್ರಣ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿದೆ.

poems.title: ಸಮರಸದ ಬದುಕು
poems.kannada_text:
ಸಮರಸದಿ ಬಾಳಬೇಕು
ಎಲ್ಲರೊಡನೆ ಬೆರೆಯಬೇಕು
ದ್ವೇಷ ಮರೆತು ಪ್ರೀತಿ ಬಿತ್ತು
ಸೌಹಾರ್ದ ಬೆಳೆಯಬೇಕು
poems.english_meaning:
We must live in harmony
We must mingle with everyone
Forget hatred and sow love
Goodwill must grow
poems.bhavartha: ಗೋಕಾಕ್ ಅವರ ಸಾಮಾಜಿಕ ತತ್ವ ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಸ್ಪಷ್ಟವಾಗಿ ಕಾಣುತ್ತದೆ.

poems.title: ಪಂಪನ ಪ್ರೇಮ
poems.kannada_text:
ಪ್ರೇಮವೇ ಜಗದ ಮೂಲ
ಪ್ರೇಮದಿ ಜಗ ತುಂಬಿದೆ
ಪ್ರೇಮ ಕಳೆದ ಜೀವ ಬರಿ ದೇಹ
ಪ್ರೇಮದಿ ಆತ್ಮ ಬದುಕಿದೆ
poems.english_meaning:
Love is the root of the world
The world is filled with love
A life without love is just a body
The soul lives through love
poems.bhavartha: ಪಂಪನ ಆದಿಪುರಾಣದ ಪ್ರೇಮ ತತ್ವ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಮೂಡಿದೆ.

poems.title: ಶಿವನ ಡಮರು
poems.kannada_text:
ಶಿವನ ಡಮರು ನಾದ ಕೇಳಿ
ಜಗವೆಲ್ಲ ನರ್ತಿಸಿತು
ಸೃಷ್ಟಿ ಸ್ಥಿತಿ ಲಯದ ನಾದ
ಬ್ರಹ್ಮಾಂಡದಲ್ಲಿ ಅನುರಣಿಸಿತು
poems.english_meaning:
Hearing the sound of Shiva's drum
The whole world danced
The sound of creation preservation and dissolution
Reverberated through the universe
poems.bhavartha: ಕಂಬಾರ ಅವರ ಭಕ್ತಿ ಮತ್ತು ತಾತ್ವಿಕ ಚಿಂತನೆ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಒಂದಾಗಿವೆ.

poems.title: ನೆಲದ ಮಗ
poems.kannada_text:
ನಾನು ನೆಲದ ಮಗ
ಮಣ್ಣಿನ ವಾಸನೆ ನನ್ನ ಉಸಿರು
ಈ ನೆಲ ನನ್ನ ತಾಯಿ
ಈ ಭೂಮಿ ನನ್ನ ದೇವರು
poems.english_meaning:
I am a son of the soil
The scent of earth is my breath
This land is my mother
This earth is my God
poems.bhavartha: ಕಂಬಾರ ಅವರ ಭೂಮಿ ಪ್ರೇಮ ಮತ್ತು ಜಾನಪದ ಪರಂಪರೆ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಒಂದಾಗಿವೆ.

poems.title: ಬದಲಾವಣೆ
poems.kannada_text:
ಬದಲಾಗದೇ ಏನೂ ಇಲ್ಲ
ಕಾಲ ಸದಾ ಬದಲಾಗುತ್ತದೆ
ಹಳೆಯ ಚಿಂತನೆ ಬಿಡಬೇಕು
ಹೊಸ ಜಗತ್ತು ಕಟ್ಟಬೇಕು
poems.english_meaning:
Nothing remains unchanged
Time always changes
We must leave old thoughts behind
We must build a new world
poems.bhavartha: ಅನಂತಮೂರ್ತಿ ಅವರ ಪ್ರಗತಿಪರ ಚಿಂತನೆ ಈ ಕವಿತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಗೋಚರಿಸುತ್ತದೆ.

poets.name: Kuvempu
poets.awards: Jnanpith Award 1967, Padma Vibhushan 1988, Rashtra Kavi, Pampa Award
poets.famous_works: Ramayana Darshanam, Malegalalli Madumagalu, Kanuru Heggadithi, Sri Ramayana Darshanam
poets.education: Mysore University — BA, MA in Kannada
poets.bio: Kuppali Venkatappa Puttappa, popularly known as Kuvempu, was a pioneering Kannada poet, novelist, playwright and critic. He was the first Kannada writer to receive the Jnanpith Award in 1967. He was also honored as the Rashtraka Kavi of Karnataka. Born in a small village Kuppali in Shivamogga district, he rose to become one of the most celebrated literary figures of India. His philosophy of Vishwamanava (Universal Human) championed humanism, progressive thinking and scientific temperament. His epic poem Sri Ramayana Darshanam is considered one of the greatest works in Kannada literature. He served as Vice Chancellor of Mysore University.

poets.name: Da. Ra. Bendre
poets.awards: Jnanpith Award 1973, Padma Shri 1968, Sahitya Akademi Award
poets.bio: Dattatreya Ramachandra Bendre, known by his pen name Ambikatanadatta, was a celebrated Kannada poet from North Karnataka. His poetry blends folk traditions with classical forms, drawing deeply from the Dharwad cultural milieu. He received the Jnanpith Award in 1973 for his collection Naadaleele. His verses are known for their musicality, spiritual depth and earthy imagery rooted in rural Karnataka. He was a teacher by profession and spent most of his life in Dharwad. His poetry in the North Karnataka dialect gave it a unique flavour that set him apart from all other Kannada poets.

poets.name: V. K. Gokak
poets.awards: Jnanpith Award 1990, Padma Shri, Sahitya Akademi Award
poets.famous_works: Bharatha Sindhu Rashmi, Samrasada Doni, Chandamama, Dyava Prithvi
poets.bio: Vinayaka Krishna Gokak was a distinguished Kannada poet, novelist and educationist. He received the Jnanpith Award in 1990 for his epic poem Bharatha Sindhu Rashmi. He was the champion of the Kannada language movement known as the Gokak Movement in 1982 which fought for the recognition of Kannada as a compulsory subject in Karnataka schools. He was Vice Chancellor of Bangalore University and Dharwad University. A scholar of international repute, he studied at Oxford and brought a global perspective to Kannada literature.

poets.name: U. R. Ananthamurthy
poets.awards: Jnanpith Award 1994, Padma Bhushan 1998, Sahitya Akademi Award
poets.bio: Udupi Rajagopalacharya Ananthamurthy was one of the most significant writers of modern Indian literature. His novel Samskara is a landmark work questioning Brahmanical orthodoxy and caste hierarchy. A towering intellectual and progressive voice, he received the Jnanpith Award in 1994. His writing explores the tension between tradition and modernity in Indian society. He served as Chairman of the National Book Trust and was the president of Sahitya Akademi. He was deeply influenced by Western existentialism and Indian philosophy.

poets.name: Girish Karnad
poets.awards: Jnanpith Award 1998, Padma Bhushan 1992, Padma Vibhushan 2019, Sahitya Akademi Award
poets.famous_works: Tughlaq, Hayavadana, Taledanda, Nagamandala, Yayati, Agni Mattu Male
poets.bio: Girish Karnad was a celebrated playwright, actor, film director and Kannada writer. He is widely regarded as one of the most important playwrights of post-independence India. His plays draw on Indian mythology, history and folklore to examine contemporary issues. He received the Jnanpith Award in 1998. He directed landmark films including Vamsha Vriksha and Kaadu. He was a Rhodes Scholar at Oxford University. His play Tughlaq written when he was just 23 is considered a masterpiece of Indian theatre. He was a vocal public intellectual who was known for his progressive views.

poets.name: Chandrashekhara Kambara
poets.awards: Jnanpith Award 2010, Padma Vibhushan 2023, Sahitya Akademi Award, Pampa Award
poets.famous_works: Siri Sampige, Jokumaraswamy, Shivalenka, Harakeya Kuri, Sangeya Honna Kereyalli
poets.bio: Chandrashekhara Kambara is a living legend of Kannada literature, theatre and folklore. A poet, playwright, novelist and film director, his works deeply celebrate North Karnataka folk traditions, music and culture. He is the founder-vice chancellor of Kannada University, Hampi. He received the Jnanpith Award in 2010. His plays like Jokumaraswamy and Siri Sampige have been performed all over the world. He is known for seamlessly weaving folk elements with modern theatrical techniques. His contribution to Kannada theatre and literature spans over five decades.

poets.name: Adikavi Pampa
poets.awards: Known as Adikavi (First Poet) of Kannada literature
poets.bio: Adikavi Pampa is considered the first and greatest poet of Kannada literature. He lived in the 10th century and wrote two masterpieces — Adipurana based on Jain philosophy and Vikramarjuna Vijaya also known as Pampa Bharata based on the Mahabharata. His literary excellence and use of the Kannada language set the foundation for all future Kannada poetry. He served in the court of Chalukya king Arikesari II. His poetry is known for its emotional depth, philosophical wisdom and beautiful use of nature imagery. He is revered as the father of Kannada literature.

poets.name: Ranna
poets.awards: One of the three gems of Kannada literature (Ratnatraya)
poets.bio: Ranna was one of the three gems of early Kannada literature alongside Pampa and Ponna. He lived in the 10th century during the Rashtrakuta and Western Chalukya periods. His masterpiece Gadayuddha describes the mace fight between Bhima and Duryodhana from the Mahabharata with extraordinary vividness and emotion. He was a court poet of the Western Chalukya king Tailapa II and later Satyashraya. His poetry is known for its heroic tone, emotional intensity and mastery of the Champu style which mixes prose and poetry. He called himself the poet of the brave (Kavichakravarthi).

poets.name: Basavanna
poets.awards: Founder of Lingayat movement, social reformer and philosopher
poets.bio: Basavanna was a 12th century philosopher, statesman, social reformer and poet. He was the Prime Minister of King Bijjala of Kalachuri dynasty in Kalyana (modern Basavakalyan). He founded the Lingayat movement which rejected caste distinctions, idol worship and ritual. His vachanas (prose poems) are considered literary masterpieces and contain deep philosophical and social messages. He advocated equality between all human beings regardless of caste or gender, the dignity of labour through Kayaka philosophy, and living with integrity. His teachings inspired generations of social reformers across India.

poets.name: Akka Mahadevi
poets.awards: One of the earliest known women poets of Karnataka
poets.famous_works: Over 430 surviving Vachanas dedicated to Chennamallikarjuna
poets.bio: Akka Mahadevi was a 12th century poet-saint and one of the earliest known women poets of Karnataka. She was a devotee of Lord Shiva whom she addressed as Chennamallikarjuna. Her vachanas express deep devotion, feminine power and rejection of worldly life. She is believed to have renounced all material possessions and wandered as a spiritual seeker. Her poetry challenges social norms and asserts the spiritual equality of women. She is considered a symbol of feminine spiritual power and courage in Karnataka. She is said to have attained liberation at Srisailam in Andhra Pradesh at a very young age.
